ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for chili crab yam dumpling. Mapped to generic dumpling class as closest defensible ancestor. Exact Singaporean chili crab yam dumpling not represented in FoodOn as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for chili crab yam dumpling. A0CKP (crustaceans, prepared/processed) used as the closest defensible anchor given crab is the primary protein component. Yam wrapper component not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for chili crab yam dumpling or sufficiently close prepared seafood dumpling as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 pieces of chili crab yam dumpling, weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served at Singaporean dim sum or seafood restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Chili Crab Yam Dumpling — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per_100g macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for Chili Crab Yam Dumpling (3 pcs), cr_id G00010690, confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous crab dumpling entries and documented below.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — taro/yam-based steamed dumpling analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chili crab yam dumpling. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on: (1) taro/yam has a reported GI of approximately 53 (boiled taro, University of Sydney GI Database); (2) the yam skin wrapper is thinner than a full taro serving and is combined with high-protein crab filling which further attenuates glycemic response; (3) protein content of 8.71 g/100g moderates postprandial glucose rise. Estimate is conservative and placed at the lower end of the medium-low boundary. GL per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 48 × 9.64 / 100 ≈ 4.6 (serving = 120 g, carbohydrate = 8.03 g/100g × 1.2 = 9.64 g).

hypertension.guidance: With only 46 mg sodium per 100 g, chili crab yam dumpling is suitable for individuals managing hypertension when consumed without additional high-sodium dipping sauces; patients should be advised to limit or avoid accompanying chili crab sauce, which can contribute substantially more sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8 g/100g), low GI (~48), high protein (8.71 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content (8.03 g/100g), estimated low GI of approximately 48, and high protein content (8.71 g/100g) make this dumpling a suitable occasional choice for individuals with type 2 diabetes; portion control is advised and additional chili crab sauce should be accounted for due to its added sugar and starch content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.9 g/100g), low sodium, moderate cholesterol (28 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat and very low sodium profile of this dumpling are favorable for cardiovascular health; the moderate cholesterol content (28 mg/100g) is within acceptable limits for most individuals with cardiovascular disease when consumed in standard serving sizes, and the crab protein provides lean amino acids without significant atherogenic fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade ingredients (chili) may trigger joint sensitivity in susceptible individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Crab meat provides lean protein and some omega-3 fatty acids which may support anti-inflammatory pathways, but individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor personal tolerance to chili and nightshade-containing sauces, as solanine compounds may aggravate joint inflammation in sensitive patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chili Crab Yam Dumpling (辣椒螃蟹芋头饺) is a Singaporean Chinese dim sum dish consisting of steamed dumplings with a skin made from yam (taro), filled with seasoned crab meat and chili crab sauce. Typically served in portions of three pieces, this dish is a creative fusion of Singapore's iconic chili crab flavour profile encased in a soft, slightly sticky yam wrapper. The texture is tender and yielding, with the yam skin providing a mild, earthy sweetness that complements the savoury, mildly spicy crab filling. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate, making it a relatively low-calorie, high-protein seafood dish. The sodium content is very low at 46 mg/100g, and the estimated glycemic index is approximately 48 (low), making it suitable for individuals managing hypertension or type 2 diabetes when consumed without additional high-sodium or high-sugar sauces. The dish contains crustacean allergens and egg, and is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. It is commonly served at Singaporean seafood restaurants and dim sum establishments as a lunch or dinner item, and reflects the culinary innovation of Singapore's Chinese food culture.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id G00010690) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries and clearly documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous taro entry with documented rationale. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.9 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for steamed crab dumplings (closest analogous entry); not directly reported by HPB for this item. 2. Dietary fibre (1.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central taro and crab dumpling analogues; not directly reported by HPB for this item. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Singaporean chili crab yam dumpling preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/absent in steamed seafood dumplings of this type; no hydrogenated fats used in traditional preparation. 5. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on boiled taro GI (~53, University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted downward for the attenuating effect of high protein content (8.71 g/100g) and the thinner yam wrapper relative to a full taro serving. 6. Halal status marked false as this dish contains crab, which is not universally accepted as halal under all Islamic jurisprudence schools; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant or producer.
